content:
{
  "market_assessment": {
    "bias": "CAUTIOUS",
    "reasoning": "NIFTY -1.05%, BankNifty -1.09%, India VIX +8.91% to 18.34 — risk-off morning with broad sector weakness except IT (+0.04%) and pockets of FMCG/defensive strength. Crude near $104 and geopolitical tensions argue against fresh aggressive admits; existing book is reasonably diversified with 65% deployed. Focus this tick: trail SLs on winners (PNBHOUSING +10.3%, COFORGE +8.8%, CAMS +2.7%) to lock gains while letting laggards run to defined SLs.",
    "key_levels": {
      "nifty_support": 23800,
      "nifty_resistance": 24100
    }
  },
  "decisions": [
    {
      "action": "MODIFY",
      "symbol": "PNBHOUSING",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "new_stop_loss": 1073.10,
      "new_target": 1135.00,
      "confidence": 0.75,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "HOLD-equivalent: position is +10.3% with SL at 1073 (already above entry, locked-in gain of ~9%). CMP 1085 only 1.1% above stored SL — trail-gap discipline (Rule, 1% floor) says do NOT tighten further; it would force same-session stopout on normal noise. Stored SL already optimal. Emitting MODIFY would be no-op — converting to HOLD via position_actions."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "GOLDBEES",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.6,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "Defensive gold ETF, -1.0%. SL 121 = 2.3% below CMP, well-placed. Geopolitical premium intact, VIX +9%. Hold thesis intact."
    },
    {
      "action": "MODIFY",
      "symbol": "MARICO",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "new_stop_loss": 820.00,
      "new_target": 855.00,
      "confidence": 0.7,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "MARICO already at SL=entry (breakeven SL). CMP 830.85 is 1.3% above SL — trail-gap floor (1%) just barely satisfied. Q4 strong, FMCG rotation active, DABUR +3.77% read-across positive. SL at entry is appropriate; no change needed — moving to HOLD via position_actions to avoid no-op MODIFY."
    },
    {
      "action": "MODIFY",
      "symbol": "COFORGE",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "new_stop_loss": 1352.00,
      "new_target": 1420.00,

      "reasoning": "+8.8% unrealized, CMP 1370 vs SL 1352 = 1.3% gap, satisfies trail-gap floor. SL already locks +7.4% gain. IT sector tailwind as defensive play. Stored SL already at optimal trail level; no-op MODIFY — converting to HOLD."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "GRSE",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.65,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "Defence -1% intraday. SL 3000 = 1.9% below CMP, properly placed. Strong FY26 numbers (+41.8% PAT) and Rs 17,000 cr order book intact. Let SL fire if hit; do not preempt (Rule 17)."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "CAMS",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.7,
      "timeframe": "SWING",

    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "PIDILITIND",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.6,
      "timeframe": "SWING",

    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "WOCKPHARMA",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.55,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "+0.8%. Research flagged NEUTRAL (-0.05 modifier) with post-results underperformance and SMP changes. SL 1580 = 1.6% below CMP 1606, properly placed. Letting SL govern."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "SRF",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",

    },
    {

      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.6,

    },
    {

      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "-2.0%. SL 10440 = 2.5% below CMP 10711. Buyback at 12000 + Q4 strong results + dividend = bullish catalysts intact. Auto sector weak today but stock-specific catalysts strong. SL respects Rule 17."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "DABUR",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.7,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "-1.0% but pre-market shows +3.77% surge on Q4 results (PAT +16%, raised FY27 guidance). SL 472 = 3.2% below CMP, well-placed. FMCG rotation tailwind. Hold."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "LAURUSLABS",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.55,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "+0.5%. Kotak Sell at 800 flagged but Q4 pending in May — binary risk. SL 1190 = 3.1% below CMP 1228. Hold to SL; do not add given Kotak overhang and pending binary."
    }
  ],
  "position_actions": [

    {

  "watchlist_notes": "Reviewed bullish-rated fresh candidates (TITAN +0.10, BAJAJ-AUTO +0.15, DABUR +0.12, ABCAPITAL +0.12, PIDILITIND +0.10, POLYCAB +0.10) — most are already held positions; new admits skipped because: (1) HFCL BUY MIS LIMIT @ 133.50 already OPEN/pending (Order Hygiene) — that order is also stale (placed 7471m / 5+ days ago), must be reviewed; (2) VIX +8.9% to 18.34 with NIFTY -1% argues against fresh admits on a risk-off morning; (3) deployment at 65% with 15 existing positions already — sector caps tight. THERMAX (+11.84% gap), NUVAMA (+10.65%) — chasing gap-ups not the right setup on risk-off day. Will reassess if NIFTY reclaims 24000 and VIX cools below 17.",
  "portfolio_notes": "STALE OPEN ORDER WARNING: HFCL BUY MIS LIMIT @ 133.50 placed 7471 minutes (~5 days) ago is still OPEN and presumably stale — MIS orders should not carry across sessions. Need to CANCEL this order; emitting CANCEL on next decision tick if engine supports, or via separate order management. Day-22, deployment 65% (within 80% soft cap), 15 holdings with diversified sector tags. Risk-off tape (VIX +8.9%, NIFTY -1%, crude $104) plus 5-day stats (12% win rate, profit factor 0.5x) argues for capital preservation today rather than fresh admits. Per Rule 18: each bullish candidate has been considered and rejected for stated reasons (already held, or risk-off macro). Tick is HOLD-heavy by design, not by anchoring."
}
